product: Use OAuth to authenticate Databricks connections in Power Platform
tagline: You can now use OAuth to authenticate a Databricks connection in Microsoft Power Platform, as an alternative to personal access tokens (PAT).
what_it_does: You can now use OAuth to authenticate a Databricks connection in Microsoft Power Platform, as an alternative to personal access tokens (PAT). This page explains how to connect to Databricks from Microsoft Power Platform by creating a Databricks connection. You can use your Databricks data to create Power canvas apps, Power Automate flows, and Copilot Studio agents.
why_it_matters: Limitations The Power Platform connector does not support government clouds.

capabilities.title: If you are using OAuth
capabilities.desc: If you are using OAuth, select AWS OAuth Connection.

capabilities.title: If you are using PAT
capabilities.desc: If you are using PAT, for API Key, enter your PAT in the format of Bearer <personal-access-token>. For Server Hostname, enter…

key_takeaway: You can now use OAuth to authenticate a Databricks connection in Microsoft Power Platform, as an alternative to personal access tokens (PAT).
